tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Hero in a Frozen Lake: Indian Worker Saves Child, Awarded Romanian Citizenship
stories.summary: Construction worker Vipan Kumar jumped into an icy lake to rescue a drowning child and was honored with Romanian citizenship by the town of Craiova.

stories.headline: Playing Through Fever, South Korea's Oh Hyeon-gyu Scores the Goal That Changed Everything
stories.summary: Striker Oh Hyeon-gyu scored the decisive 80th-minute goal for South Korea while battling a 38°C fever, transforming a tense World Cup match into a stunning comeback victory.

stories.headline: Eleven-Year-Old Mowing His Way Through a Mission of Kindness
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old Braden of Arkansas City, Kansas is mowing 50 yards for free this summer through the Raising Men & Women Lawn Care Service to help elderly, disabled, and veteran neighbors.

stories.headline: Life Saved at the Allotment: Strangers' CPR Reunites Them in Gratitude
stories.summary: When 61-year-old Anne Sutton collapsed in cardiac arrest at her East Leake allotment, fellow gardeners Stacey Wardle and nurse Adele Lapworth sprang into action, performing life-saving CPR that brought her back.

stories.headline: Spilled Treasure: Crashed Truck Full of Cucumbers Feeds Thousands Across Wisconsin
stories.summary: A truck driver and company transformed nearly 40,000 pounds of produce into a gift for hungry families across Wisconsin, turning accident into abundance.

stories.headline: MIT's Water-from-Air Breakthrough Offers Desert Communities a Lifeline
stories.summary: Ultrasonic technology pulls drinking water from air 45 times faster than solar methods, bringing hope to arid regions worldwide.

stories.headline: First Glimpse in Five Years: Rare White Abalone Spotted, Sparking Hope for Recovery
stories.summary: Marine scientists recorded a live white abalone during a May 12, 2026 survey in California's Channel Islands—the first confirmed sighting in years, sparking hope for the endangered sea snail's recovery.

stories.headline: Cut-and-Patch Genetic Medicine Offers Hope for Neurodegenerative Disease
stories.summary: Hong Kong scientists developed RNA Segment Editing—a precise tool that corrects faulty genetic messages without permanently altering DNA.

stories.headline: Rare Caracal Caught on Camera: India's Grassland Protectors Show Success
stories.summary: The elusive Caracal, called the 'ghost of the grasslands,' was recently captured on camera trap in Kuno National Park, Madhya Pradesh, marking a rare sighting after decades that proves habitat protection is working.
